source.name: OWID COVID-19 Dataset — Weekly excess mortality for selected countries
references: Mathieu et al. (2021) Nature Human Behaviour 5:947-953

columns.proxy_description: 1 - excess_mortality_pct_norm: inverse of excess mortality. High O = health system maintaining normal mortality = organized.
columns.fragility_note: Excess mortality depends on baseline calculation method.
columns.manipulability_note: Mortality data from national statistical offices; difficult to manipulate.

columns.proxy_description: ICU spare capacity (1 - icu_occupancy_rate). High R = resilient health system with buffer capacity.
columns.fragility_note: ICU data reporting varies by country.

columns.proxy_description: Vaccination coverage rate (% fully vaccinated). High I = integrated public health response.
columns.fragility_note: Vaccination data quality varies by country.

columns.proxy_description: New cases per million (7-day smoothed, log-transformed). Pandemic demand pressure on health system.
columns.fragility_note: Case counts depend on testing rates; more reliable post-2021.

columns.proxy_description: Cumulative months below 10% excess mortality — symbolic stock of health system stability.
